original_prompt: How many appointments do I have on May 15th, 2024? Please give me a summary of the description for each appointment.
assessment_reasoning: The user request is explicit: it specifies an exact date (May 15, 2024) and asks for two clear outputs — the number of appointments and a summary of the description for each. This directly maps to a single calendar lookup for that day. There are no missing constraints or unclear goals; at most minor unstated details (time zones, whether to include canceled/private events) could be considered, but they are not required to fulfill the straightforward task. Therefore the prompt is extremely clear.

variations.L2: Am I booked on May 15? If so, how many appointments do I have and what are they?
variations.L3: How many appointments do I have on May 15th, 2024? Please give me a short summary of the description for each appointment.
variations.L4: Count my appointments on May 15, 2024 (calendar in my default account) and provide a brief one-line summary of the description for each appointment. Exclude canceled events. Return the total first, then the list.
variations.L5: Call get_day_calendar_events with day='2024-05-15'. Return plain text where the first line is the total number of appointments (an integer). Then, output a numbered list where each item is a one-line summary (20–30 words) of that appointment's description. Do not include any other metadata.
